generic_rules: Focus repairs on resolving AGREE syntax errors and unresolved identifiers.
Prioritize repairs that maintain architectural consistency; avoid changes outside the immediate scope of the diagnostic.
When a diagnostic indicates a scope error, consider moving the contract to the appropriate scope (type vs. implementation).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `int`, `real`, and `bool` for scalar type declarations.
Guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce unqualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not attempt to fix type errors by modifying the AADL architecture.
